L1.area: Asynchronous task management and execution primitives

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a lifetime bound is missing on a generic type parameter in an API that converts a reference-counted smart pointer into an asynchronous task notification handle. Non-static data can be used where a static lifetime is required, and if the handle is invoked after the original data has been dropped, it results in a use-after-free.

L3.area: Asynchronous task waking utilities and reference-counted wake handle construction
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where a lifetime bound is missing on a generic type parameter in a function that constructs an asynchronous task wake handle from an Arc-wrapped type. Non-static data can be used where a static lifetime is required, and if the wake handle is invoked after the original data has been dropped, it results in a use-after-free.

ground_truth.reason: The waker function did not enforce a 'static lifetime bound on its generic type parameter, allowing non-static data to be transmuted into a Waker via Arc, resulting in a use-after-free when the Waker is invoked after the original data is dropped.
